preamble.summary: A brief continuation of the vision of restoration: all will worship God in righteousness, the earth will be purified, and blessing will rest upon it forever.
preamble.remarkable: This short chapter functions as a coda to the judgment and restoration sequence of chapter 10, bringing the section to a close before the narrative shifts to Enoch's personal involvement.
preamble.friction: None significant. The chapter's brevity suggests it may have originally been part of chapter 10.

verses.text_reference: And in those days I will open the store chambers of blessing which are in the heaven, so as to send them down upon the earth over the work and labour of the children of men.
verses.rendering: And in those days I will open the storehouses of blessing that are in heaven, so as to send them down upon the earth, upon the work and labor of the children of humanity.
translator_notes: 'Storehouses of blessing in heaven' echoes Deuteronomy 28:12 ('the LORD will open to you his good treasury, the heavens') and Malachi 3:10 ('open the windows of heaven and pour down a blessing').

verses.text_reference: And truth and peace shall be associated together throughout all the days of the world and throughout all the generations of men.
verses.rendering: And truth and peace will be joined together throughout all the days of the world and throughout all the generations of humanity.
translator_notes: 'Truth and peace joined together' echoes Psalm 85:10 ('steadfast love and faithfulness meet; righteousness and peace kiss each other'). The pairing suggests that lasting peace requires truth as its foundation.
